event: The Harrowing of Hell or souls tormented in Limbo
symbols: bat-winged horned demon — Satan or a chief devil presiding over the realm of the dead
nude tormented figures — the vulnerability of souls stripped of all earthly protection, exposed to spiritual suffering
chains and bonds — the bondage of sin and death from which Christ liberates the captives
dark cavernous space — Sheol or the underworld, the realm of the dead before Christ's victory
figures reaching upward — the longing of the righteous dead for deliverance, awaiting the promised Redeemer
description: A monochrome drawing or print in silverpoint or pen, depicting a scene of infernal torment. At the left, a large horned demon with bat-like wings crouches or sits, gripping or restraining figures near it. In the center, a nude male figure twists in agony, arms thrown back, while other writhing bodies surround him. At the right edge, additional skeletal or emaciated figures appear, some in chains or bonds. The entire scene takes place in a dark, enclosed cavern-like space. The drawing style is precise and detailed, with fine hatching creating tonal depth.
theological_description: This image depicts the infernal realm -- whether understood as Hell, Hades, or Limbo -- where souls suffer under demonic dominion. The Christian tradition of Christ's descent into Hell (the Harrowing of Hell), based on 1 Peter 3:18-20 and the Apostles' Creed ('He descended into Hell'), holds that between his death and resurrection, Christ entered the realm of the dead to liberate the righteous who had died before his coming. Ephesians 4:8-9 declares that 'when he ascended on high he led a host of captives,' interpreted by patristic writers as Christ's triumphant liberation of Adam, Eve, the patriarchs, and prophets from the power of death. The demonic figures embody the 'rulers and authorities' whom Christ 'disarmed' and 'put to open shame' through the cross (Colossians 2:15). The suffering depicted also evokes Jesus' parable of the rich man and Lazarus (Luke 16:19-31), in which the rich man suffers torment in Hades while Abraham and Lazarus rest in comfort. Revelation 20:13-14 prophesies the ultimate destruction of Death and Hades themselves, cast into the lake of fire -- the final defeat of the demonic powers depicted in this scene.

scripture_refs.reason: Christ went and proclaimed to the spirits in prison -- the scriptural foundation for the Harrowing of Hell depicted here.
Christ descended into the lower regions and led captivity captive, liberating souls from the infernal realm.
The demons depicted here represent the rulers and authorities Christ disarmed and put to open shame through the cross.
The tormented figures in the infernal realm evoke the rich man's suffering in Hades in Jesus' parable.
The prophesied destruction of Death and Hades provides the eschatological horizon for this scene of infernal suffering.
